steps.title: Identify what the opening phrase logically needs
steps.content: The phrase “Supported by biochemical analyses...” describes something being supported by scientific evidence. This opening must logically connect to a subject. What does evidence logically “support” — the physical items being studied, or the conclusions drawn about them?

steps.title: Find the subject that evidence logically supports
steps.content: Choice D uses “findings from a 2022 study” as the subject — findings are what biochemical evidence logically “supports” or validates. Choices A, B, and C use items (vegetables, diets, components) as subjects — these are objects that evidence analyzes, not things that evidence “supports.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
